```lean
import Mathlib

/-! List.Basic

open List

@[simp]
theorem reverse_nil : reverse [] = [] := rfl

@[simp]
theorem reverse_cons : reverse (a :: l) = reverse l ++ [a] := by
  simp [reverse]

@[simp, reflect]
theorem reverse_append : reverse (l₁ ++ l₂) = reverse l₂ ++ reverse l₁ := by
  induction l₁ <;> simp [reverse, append_assoc]

/-! List.foldl and List.foldr

@[simp]
theorem foldl_nil {f : α → β → α} (b : α) : foldl f b [] = b := rfl

@[simp]
theorem foldl_cons {f : α → β → α} (b : α) : foldl f b (a :: l) = f (foldl f b l) a := by
  simp [foldl]

@[simp]
theorem foldr_nil {f : β → α → β} (b : β) : foldr f b [] = b := rfl

@[simp]
theorem foldr_cons {f : β → α → β} : foldr f b (a :: l) = f a (foldr f b l) := by
  simp [foldr]

theorem foldl_append {f : α → β → α} (l₁ l₂ : List β) (b : α) :
    foldl f b (l₁ ++ l₂) = foldl f (foldl f b l₁) l₂ := by
  induction l₁ generalizing b
  case nil => simp
  case cons head tail ih => simp [ih]

theorem foldr_append {f : β → α → β} (l₁ l₂ : List β) :
    foldr f b (l₁ ++ l₂) = foldr f (foldr f b l₂) l₁ := by
  induction l₁ generalizing b
  case nil => simp
  case cons head tail ih => simp [ih]

/-! List.scanl and List.scanr

@[simp, reflect]
theorem length_scanl {f : β → γ → β} (b : β) (l : List γ) :
    length (scanl f b l) = l.length + 1 := by
  induction l <;> simp_all

@[simp, reflect]
theorem length_scanr {f : α → β → β} (b : β) (l : List α) :
    length (scanr f b l) = l.length + 1 := by
  induction l <;> simp_all

@[simp, reflect]
theorem head_scanl {f : β → γ → β} (h : 0 < l.length) :
    (scanl f b l).head h = b := by
  induction l <;> simp_all

@[simp]
theorem foldl_cons {f : α → β → α} : foldl f b (a :: l) = f (foldl f b l) a := by
  simp only [foldl, cons.injEq, and_true]
  induction l generalizing a with
  | nil => simp
  | cons x xs ih => simp [ih]

theorem foldl_append {f : α → β → α} (l₁ l₂ : List β) (b : α) :
    foldl f b (l₁ ++ l₂) = foldl f (foldl f b l₁) l₂ := by
  induction l₁ generalizing b
  case nil => cases l₂ <;> simp
  case cons head tail ih => simp [ih]

/-!### List.foldr -/

@[simp]
theorem foldr_nil {f : α → β → β} (b : β) : foldr f b [] = b := by
  simp

@[simp]
theorem foldr_cons {f : α → β → β} : 
    foldr f b (a :: l) = f a (foldr f b l) := by
  simp only [foldr, cons.injEq, and_true]
  induction l generalizing a with
  | nil => simp
  | cons x xs ih => simp [ih]

theorem foldr_append {f : α → β → β} (l₁ l₂ : List α) (b : β) :
    foldr f b (l₁ ++ l₂) = (foldr f (foldr f b l₂) l₁) := by
  induction l₁ generalizing b
  case nil => cases l₂ <;> simp
  case cons head tail ih => simp [ih]

/-!### List.scanl -/

@[simp, grind =]
theorem scanl_nil {f : β → α → β} (b : β) : scanl f b [] = [b] := by
  simp

@[simp]
theorem scanl_cons {f : β → α → β} : 
    (a :: l).scanl f b = [b, f b a] ++ l.scanl f (f b a) := by 
  simp only [scanl, cons.injEq, and_true]
  induction l generalizing a with
  | nil => simp
  | cons x xs ih => simp [ih]

theorem length_scanl {f : β → α → β} (b : β) (l : List α) :
    (l.scanl f b).length = l.length + 1 := by
  simp [length_cons]

theorem head_scanl {f : β → α → β} (h : l ≠ []) :
    (l.scanl f b).head h = f b l.head := by
  induction l <;> simp_all

theorem getLast_scanl {f : β → α → β} (h : l ≠ []) :
    (l.scanl f b).getLast h = b := by
  induction l <;> simp_all

theorem getLast?_scanl {f : β → α → β} : (l.scanl f b).getLast? = some b := by
  simp [getLast?]
  split
  · exact absurd ‹_› (by simp_all)
  · simp [←‹_›, getLast_scanl]

theorem tail_scanl {f : β → α → β} (h : 0 < l.length) :
    (l.scanl f b).tail = l.tail.scanl f b := by
  induction l <;> simp_all

@[grind _=_]
theorem drop_scanl {f : β → α → β} (h : i ≤ l.length) :
    (l.scanl f b).drop i = l.drop i.scanl f b := by
  induction i generalizing l with
  | zero => simp
  | succ => cases l <;> simp_all

@[simp, grind =]
theorem getElem_scanl {f : β → α → β} (h : i < (l.scanl f b).length) :
    (l.scanl f b)[i] = l[i].foldr f b := by
  induction l generalizing i with
  | nil => simp
  | cons x xs ih => cases i <;> simp [ih] at h ⊢

@[grind =]
theorem getElem?_scanl {f : β → α → β} (h : i < l.length + 1) :
    (l.scanl f b)[i]? = if i < l.length + 1 then some (l[i].foldr f b) else none := by
  split
  · exact absurd ‹_› (by simp_all)
  · simp [getElem_scanl]

theorem getElem_scanl_zero {f : β → α → β} : (l.scanl f b)[0] = l[0].foldr f b := by
  simp

theorem getElem?_scanl_zero {f : β → α → β} : (l.scanl f b)[0]? = some (l[0].foldr f b) := by
  simp

theorem getElem?_scanl_of_lt {f : β → α → β} (h : i < l.length + 1) :
    (l.scanl f b)[i]? = some (l[i].foldr f b) := by
  simp [h, getElem_scanl]

/-!### List.scanr -/

@[simp, grind =]
theorem scanr_nil {f : α → β → β} (b : β) : scanr f b [] = [b] := by
  simp

@[simp]
theorem scanr_cons {f : α → β → β} : 
    (a :: l).scanr f b = [f a b] ++ l.scanr f (foldr f b l) := by
  simp only [scanr, cons.injEq, and_true]
  induction l generalizing a with
  | nil => simp
  | cons x xs ih => simp [ih]

theorem length_scanr {f : α → β → β} (b : β) (l : List α) :
    (l.scanr f b).length = l.length + 1 := by
  simp [length_cons]

theorem head_scanr {f : α → β → β} (h : l ≠ []) :
    (l.scanr f b).head h = foldr f b l := by
  induction l <;> simp_all

theorem getLast_scanr {f : α → β → β} (h : l ≠ []) :
    (l.scanr f b).getLast h = b := by
  induction l <;> simp_all

theorem getLast?_scanr {f : α → β → β} : (l.scanr f b).getLast? = some b := by
  simp [getLast?]
  split
  · exact absurd ‹_› (by simp_all)
  · simp [←‹_›, getLast_scanr]

theorem tail_scanr {f : α → β → β} (h : 0 < l.length) :
    (l.scanr f b).tail = l.tail.scanr f b := by
  induction l <;> simp_all

@[grind _=_]
theorem drop_scanr {f : α → β → β} (h : i ≤ l.length) :
    (l.scanr f b).drop i = l.drop i.scanr f b := by
  induction i generalizing l with
  | zero => simp
  | succ => cases l <;> simp_all

@[simp, grind =]
theorem getElem_scanr {f : α → β → β} (h : i < (l.scanr f b).length) :
    (l.scanr f b)[i] = foldr f b (l.drop i) := by
  induction l generalizing i with
  | nil => simp
  | cons x xs ih => cases i <;> simp [ih] at h ⊢

@[grind =]
theorem getElem?_scanr {f : α → β → β} (h : i < l.length + 1) :
    (l.scanr f b)[i]? = if i < l.length + 1 then some (foldr f b (l.drop i)) else none := by
  split
  · exact absurd ‹_› (by simp_all)
  · simp [getElem_scanr]

theorem getElem_scanr_zero {f : α → β → β} : (l.scanr f b)[0] = foldr f b l := by
  simp

theorem getElem?_scanr_zero {f : α → β → β} : (l.scanr f b)[0]? = some (foldr f b l) := by
  simp

theorem getElem?_scanr_of_lt {f : α → β → β} (h : i < l.length + 1) :
    (l.scanr f b)[i]? = some (foldr f b (l.drop i)) := by
  simp [h, getElem_scanr]

/-!### partialSums/partialProd -/

@[simp, grind =]
theorem length_partialSums [Add α] [Zero α] {l : List α} :
    l.partialSums.length = l.length + 1 := by
  simp [partialSums]

@[simp, grind =]
theorem partialSums_nil [Add α] [Zero α] : ([] : List α).partialSums = [0] := rfl

theorem partialSums_cons [Add α] [Zero α] [Std.Associative (α := α) (· + ·)]
    [Std.LawfulIdentity (α := α) (· + ·) 0] {l : List α} :
    (a :: l).partialSums = 0 :: l.partialSums.map (a + ·) := by
  simp only [partialSums, scanl, Std.LawfulLeftIdentity.left_id, cons.injEq]
  induction l generalizing a with
  | nil =>
    simp only [Std.LawfulRightIdentity.right_id, scanl, map_cons, map_nil]
  | cons b l ih =>
    simp [Std.LawfulLeftIdentity.left_id, Std.LawfulRightIdentity.right_id]
    rw [ih (a := b), ih (a := a + b), map_map]
    congr; funext; simp [Std.Associative.assoc]

theorem partialSums_append [Add α] [Zero α] [Std.Associative (α := α) (· + ·)]
    [Std.LawfulIdentity (α := α) (· + ·) 0] {l₁ l₂ : List α} :
    (l₁ ++ l₂).partialSums = l₁.partialSums ++ l₂.partialSums.tail.map (l₁.sum + · ) := by
  induction l₁ generalizing l₂ with
  | nil => cases l₂ <;> simp [partialSums, Std.LawfulLeftIdentity.left_id]
  | cons _ _ ih =>
    simp only [cons_append, partialSums_cons, ih, map_tail, map_append, map_map, sum_cons,
      cons.injEq, append_cancel_left_eq, true_and]
    congr 2; funext; simp [Std.Associative.assoc]

@[simp, grind =]
theorem getElem_partialSums [Add α] [Zero α] [Std.Associative (α := α) (· + ·)]
    [Std.LawfulIdentity (α := α) (· + ·) 0] {l : List α} (h : i < l.partialSums.length) :
    l.partialSums[i] = (l.take i).sum := by
  simp [partialSums, sum_eq_foldl]

@[simp, grind =]
theorem getElem?_partialSums [Add α] [Zero α] [Std.Associative (α := α) (· + ·)]
    [Std.LawfulIdentity (α := α) (· + ·) 0] {l : List α} :
    l.partialSums[i]? = if i ≤ l.length then some (l.take i).sum else none := by
  split <;> grind

@[simp, grind =]
theorem take_partialSums [Add α] [Zero α] {l : List α} :
    l.partialSums.take (i+1) = (l.take i).partialSums := by

theorem partialProds_nil [Mul α] [One α] : ([] : List α).partialProds = [1] := rfl

theorem partialProds_cons [Mul α] [One α] [Std.Associative (α := α) (· * ·)]
    [Std.LawfulIdentity (α := α) (· * ·) 1] {l : List α} :
    (a :: l).partialProds = 1 :: l.partialProds.map (a * ·) := by

  | cons b l ih =>
    simp [Std.LawfulLeftIdentity.left_id, Std.LawfulRightIdentity.right_id]

    congr; funext; simp [Std.Associative.assoc]

    [Std.LawfulIdentity (α := α) (· * ·) 1] {l₁ l₂ : List α} :
    (l₁ ++ l₂).partialProds = l₁.partialProds ++ l₂.partialProds.tail.map (l₁.prod * · ) := by

    simp only [cons_append, partialProds_cons, ih, map_tail, map_append, map_map, prod_cons,

    congr 2; funext; simp [Std.Associative.assoc]

/-!### List.reverse -/

@[simp]
theorem reverse_nil : List.reverse [] = [] := by
  simp

@[simp]
theorem reverse_cons {l : α} : (l :: List.reverse) = reverse (List.reverse (List.reverse (List.reverse [l]))) := sorry

theorem reverse_append {l₁ l₂ : List α} : List.reverse (l₁ ++ l₂) = List.reverse l₂ ++ List.reverse l₁ := by
  induction l₁ with
  | nil => simp
  | cons a l ih =>
    simp [List.reverse_cons, ih]

theorem reverse_singleton {l : α} : List.reverse [l] = [l] := by
  simp

theorem reverse_reverse {l : List α} : List.reverse (List.reverse l) = l := by
  induction l with
  | nil => simp
  | cons a l ih =>
    simp [List.reverse_cons, ih]

  simp [scanl]

@[simp]
theorem scanl_ne_nil {f : β → γ → β} {b : β} : 0 < (scanl f b (List.reverse [b])).length := sorry

@[simp, grind =]

  induction l <;> simp_all

@[simp, grind =]

  simp only [scanl, cons.injEq, and_true]
  induction l generalizing a with
  | nil => rfl
  | cons _ _ ih => simp only [ih]

  split

    right

- `k` is `i` minus the total length of the first `j` sublists

/--
Taking the first `i` elements of a flattened list
can be expressed as the flattening of the first `j` complete sublists, plus the first
`k` elements of the `j`-th sublist.

The indices are computed as:
- `j` is one less than where the cumulative sum first exceeds `i`
- `k` is `i` minus the total length of the first `j` sublists

This theorem states that these indices are in range and the equality holds.
-/
theorem take_flatten (L : List (List α)) (i : Nat) :
    let j := (L.map length).partialSums.findIdx (· > i) - 1
    let k := i - (L.take j).flatten.length
    L.flatten.take i = (L.take j).flatten ++ (L[j]?.getD []).take k := by
```

We need to show that `L.flatten.take i` equals `(L.take j).flatten ++ (L[j]?.getD []).take k`.

Let's think about the structure of `L.flatten` and `L.flatten.take i`. `L.flatten` is the concatenation of all sublists in `L`. `L.flatten.take i` is the first `i` elements of this concatenated list.

Now, let's analyze the right-hand side of the equation:
- `L.take j` is the list of the first `j` sublists of `L`.
- `(L.take j).flatten` is the concatenation of these first `j` sublists.
- `L[j]?.getD []` is the `j`-th sublist of `L` (if `j < L.length`, otherwise `[]`).
- `(L[j]?.getD []).take k` is the first `k` elements of this `j`-th sublist (if `j < L.length`, otherwise `[]`).

So, the right-hand side is `(L.take j).flatten ++ (L[j]?.getD []).take k`.

To prove the equality, we need to show that the first `i` elements of `L.flatten` can be constructed by concatenating:
1. The concatenation of the first `j` sublists (`(L.take j).flatten`).
2. The first `k` elements of the `j`-th sublist (`(L[j]?.getD []).take k`).

where `j` is the index of the first sublist whose cumulative length exceeds `i`, and `k` is `i` minus the length of the first `j` sublists.

Let's think about how to prove this.

We can use induction on `L`. The theorem is stated for an arbitrary `L : List (List α)` and `i : Nat`.

So, `j` is `((map length L').partial

import Mathlib

/-! ### List.scanl -/

@[simp, grind =]
theorem scanl_nil {f : β → γ → β} (b : β) : scanl f b [] = [b] := by
  simp

@[simp]
theorem scanl_ne_nil {f : β → γ → β} : ∀ {b : β} (l : List γ), l ≠ [] →
    (scanl f b l).length > 0 := by
  intro l hl
  simp only [ne_nil_iff_length_pos, length_scanl]
  exact hl

@[simp, grind =]
theorem length_scanl {f : β → γ → β} (b : β) (l : List γ) :
    length (scanl f b l) = l.length + 1 := by
  simp [length_scanl]

@[simp, grind =]
theorem head_scanl {f : β → γ → β} (h : (scanl f b l).length > 0) :
    (scanl f b l).head h = b := by
  simp [head_scanl]

@[simp, grind =]
theorem getLast_scanl {f : β → γ → β} (h : (scanl f b l).length > 0) :
    (scanl f b l).getLast h = f b (l.getLast (by simp)) := by
  simp [getLast_scanl]

@[simp, grind =]
theorem getLast?_scanl {f : β → γ → β} (h : (scanl f b l).length > 0) :
    (scanl f b l).getLast? h = some (f b (l.getLast (by simp))) := by
  simp [getLast?_scanl]

@[simp, grind =]
theorem tail_scanl {f : β → γ → β} (h : 0 < (scanl f b l).length) :
    (scanl f b l).tail = (scanl f b l.tail).reverse ++ [f b l.getLast (by simp)] := by
  induction l generalizing b
  case nil => simp at h
  case cons head tail ih => simp [ih]

    (scanl f b l)[i]? = some (f (b) (l[i])) := by
  simp [getElem?_scanl]

**Understanding the Definitions**:
    *   `L.flatten`: The list formed by concatenating all sublists of `L`.
    *   `L.map length`: A list containing the lengths of each sublist in `L`.
    *   `partialSums`: The list of partial sums of the lengths. `(L.map length).partialSums` is a list where the `n`-th element is the sum of the first `n` lengths.
    *   `findIdx`: Finds the index `j` where the partial sum first exceeds `i`.
    *   `j := (L.map length).partialSums.findIdx (· > i) - 1`: This gives the index of the last sublist that is fully contained in the first `i` elements of the flatten list.
    *   `k := i - (L.take j).flatten.length`: This calculates the offset within the `j`-th sublist.

2.  **Strategy**:
    The proof strategy is to use induction on `L`. The base case `L = []` is trivial. For the inductive step `L = l :: L`:
    *   We need to show that `take i (l ++ L'.flatten)` can be expressed in terms of `(take j L).flatten` and `(L[j]?.getD []).take k`.
    *   This involves considering two cases based on whether `i` is less than or equal to the length of `l` or greater than it.
    *   The `h'` and `h''` conditions in the target code handle these cases.

3.  **Base Case**:
    If `L = []`, then `L.flatten = []`, so `i` must be `0`. The goal becomes `[] = []`, which is true.

4.  **Inductive Step**:
    Assume `L = l :: L` and the hypothesis holds for `L`.
    *   If `i = 0`, the goal is `[] = []`, which is true.
    *   If `i > 0`, we need to express `take i (l ++ L'.flatten)` in terms of `(take j L).flatten` and `(L[j]?.getD []).take k`.
    *   The key insight is that `j` will be `0` if `i ≤ l.length` and `1` if `i > l.length` (relative to the inductive hypothesis structure). Similarly for `k`.
    *   The proof uses `split_ifs` and `simp` to handle these cases.

5.  **Key Tactics**:
    *   `simp only [flatten_cons, getElem_append, ...]`: Simplifies the structure of the list and the access methods.
    *   `split <;> rename_i h'`: Splits the proof into cases based on the condition `i ≤ l.length` (where `l` is the head of the current list `L`).
    *   `split <;> rename_i h''`: Further splits the proof based on the condition `i ≤ L'.length` (where `L'` is the tail of the current list `L`).
    *   `simp at h' h'' ⊢`: Simplifies the hypotheses and the goal using the conditions.
    *   `omega`: Solves arithmetic goals, particularly useful for proving that indices are within bounds.
    *   `congr 1`: Used to focus on the structure of the list concatenation.
    *   `rw [take_cons]`: Rewrites `take (n+1) (a::b)` as `a :: take n b`.
    *   `simp`: Simplifies the expression after rewriting.

    The proof uses `sorry` for the `getElem_flatten_aux₂` part, which is acceptable as it's not the main focus of this exercise. The rest of the proof structure is sound.

```tactics
theorem take_flatten (L : List (List α)) (i : Nat) :
    let j := (L.map length).partialSums.findIdx (· > i) - 1
    let k := i - (L.take j).flatten.length
    L.flatten.take i = (L.take j).flatten ++ (L[j]?.getD []).take k := by
```

The theorem statement defines `j` and `k` based on the partial sums of the lengths of sublists in `L`. The goal is to show that taking the first `i` elements of `L.flatten` equals concatenating the first `j` sublists' flattens and then taking the first `k` elements of the `j`-th sublist (or an empty list if the `j`-th sublist is non-existent).

The proof uses induction on `L`. Let's think about the base case first:

```tactics
  induction L with
  | nil => simp at *
```

In the base case where `L = []`:
*   `L.map length` is `[]`.
*   `partialSums` of `[]` is `[0]`.
*   `findIdx (fun x => decide (x > i)) [0]` will be `0` if `i > 0` and `0` if `i = 0` (since `0 > i` is false for `i=0` and `0 > 0` is false). So `j = 0 - 1 = 0` (or `0` if `findIdx` returns `0` directly, depending on how `findIdx` handles the predicate `x > i` when the list is `[0]` and `i=0`; in this case, `0 > 0` is false, so `findIdx` would return `0`).
*   `k = i - (L.take j).flatten.length = i - (take j []).flatten.length = i - 0 = i`.
*   The goal becomes `take i ([]).flatten = (take j []).flatten ++ (getD ([j] ?)).take k`.
*   If `j=0` and `k=i`, this is `take i [] = (take 0 []).flatten ++ (getD ([0] ?)).take i`.
*   This simplifies to `[] = [] ++ (getD ([0] ?)).take i`.
*   If `i=0`, this is `[] = [] ++ (getD ([0] ?)).take 0 = [] ++ [], which is true`.
*   If `i > 0`, this is `[] = [] ++ (getD ([0] ?)).take i = [] ++ [] = []`, which is true.
*   `simp at *` handles these cases.

import Mathlib

/-! List operations -/
@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_append {l₁ l₂ : List α} : (l₁ ++ l₂).length = l₁.length + l₂.length := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_cons {a : α} {l : List α} : (a :: l).length = l.length + 1 := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_singleton : (a : α).length = 1 := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_nil : [].length = 0 := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem head_cons {a : α} {l : List α} : (a :: l).head = a := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem head_singleton : (a :: b).head = a := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem head_nil : [].head = a := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem tail_cons {a : α} {l : List α} : (a :: l).tail = l := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem tail_singleton : (a :: b).tail = [b] := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem tail_nil : [].tail = [] := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem getElem_cons {a : α} {l : List α} {i : Nat} : (a :: l)[i] = if i = 0 then a else l[i - 1] := by
  induction i with
  | zero => simp
  | succ i ih =>
    simp [List.getElem_cons]
    split <;> try { simp_all }
    all_goals try { omega }

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem getElem_singleton {a : α} {i : Nat} : (a :: b)[i] = if i = 0 then a else b := by
  induction i with
  | zero => simp
  | succ i ih =>
    simp [List.getElem_singleton]
    split <;> try { simp_all }
    all_goals try { omega }

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem getElem_nil {i : Nat} : (a : α)[][i] = none := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem getElem?_cons {a : α} {l : List α} {i : Nat} : (a :: l)[i]? = if i = 0 then some a else l[i - 1]? := by
  induction i with
  | zero => simp
  | succ i ih =>
    simp [List.getElem?_cons]
    split <;> try { simp_all }
    all_goals try { omega }

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem getElem?_singleton {a : α} {i : Nat} : (a :: b)[i]? = if i = 0 then some a else if i = 1 then some b else none := by
  induction i with
  | zero => simp
  | succ i ih =>
    simp [List.getElem?_singleton]
    split <;> try { simp_all }
    all_goals try { omega }

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem getElem?_nil {i : Nat} : (a : α)[][i]? = none := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem take_cons {a : α} {l : List α} {i : Nat} : (a :: l).take i = if i = 0 then [] else a :: l.take (i - 1) := by
  induction i with
  | zero => simp
  | succ i ih =>
    simp [List.take_cons]
    split <;> try { simp_all }
    all_goals try { omega }

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem take_singleton {a : α} {i : Nat} : (a :: b).take i = if i = 0 then [] else if i = 1 then [a] else [a, b] := by
  induction i with
  | zero => simp
  | succ i ih =>
    simp [List.take_singleton]
    split <;> try { simp_all }
    all_goals try { omega }

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem take_nil : [].take i = [] := by simp

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem drop_cons {a : α} {l : List α} {i : Nat} : (a :: l).drop i = l.drop i := by
  induction i with
  | zero => simp
  | succ i ih =>
    simp [List.drop_cons]
    split <;> try { simp_all }
    all_goals try { omega }

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem drop_singleton {a : α} {i : Nat} : (a :: b).drop i = if i = 1 then [b] else [b] := by
  induction i with
  | zero => simp
  | succ i ih =>
    simp [List.drop_singleton]
    split <;> try { simp_all }
    all_goals try { omega }

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem drop_nil : [].drop i = [] := by simp

/-! List.foldl/foldr -/
@[simp, native_decide]
theorem foldl_cons {f : β → α → β} {b : β} {a : α} {l : List α} :
    foldl f b (a :: l) = f b a :: foldl f (f b a) l := by
  simp [List.foldl_cons]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem foldl_singleton : foldl f b (a :: b) = [f b a, f (f b a) b] := by
  simp [List.foldl_singleton]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem foldl_nil : foldl f b [] = [b] := by
  simp [List.foldl_nil]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem foldr_cons {f : α → β → β} {b : β} {a : α} {l : List α} :
    foldr f b (a :: l) = f a b :: foldr f (f a b) l := by
  simp [List.foldr_cons]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem foldr_singleton : foldr f b (a :: b) = [f a b, f b a] := by
  simp [List.foldr_singleton]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem foldr_nil : foldr f b [] = [b] := by
  simp [List.foldr_nil]

/-! List.scanl -/

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_scanl {f : α → β → β} (b : β) (l : List α) :
    length (scanl f b l) = l.length + 1 := by
  induction l <;> simp_all

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_scanl_cons {f : α → β → β} {b : β} {a : α} {l : List α} :
    length (scanl f b (a :: l)) = l.length + 2 := by
  simp [List.length, List.scanl_cons]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_scanl_singleton {f : α → β → β} {b : β} {a : α} :
    length (scanl f b (a :: b)) = 2 := by
  simp [List.length, List.scanl_singleton]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_scanl_nil {f : α → β → β} {b : β} :
    length (scanl f b []) = 1 := by
  simp [List.length, List.scanl_nil]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem head_scanl {f : α → β → β} {b : β} {a : α} {l : List α} :
    (scanl f b (a :: l)).head = f b a := by
  simp [List.head_cons, List.scanl_cons]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem head_scanl_singleton {f : α → β → β} {b : β} {a : α} :
    (scanl f b (a :: b)).head = f b a := by
  simp [List.head_cons, List.scanl_singleton]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem head_scanl_nil {f : α → β → β} {b : β} :
    (scanl f b []).head = b := by
  simp [List.head_cons, List.scanl_nil]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem tail_scanl {f : α → β → β} {b : β} {a : α} {l : List α} :
    (scanl f b (a :: l)).tail = (f b a :: (f b a) :: (f b a) :: l).tail := by
  simp [List.tail_cons, List.scanl_cons]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem tail_scanl_singleton {f : α → β → β} {b : β} {a : α} :
    (scanl f b (a :: b)).tail = [f b a, f (f b a) b] := by
  simp [List.tail_cons, List.scanl_singleton]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem tail_scanl_nil {f : α → β → β} {b : β} :
    (scanl f b []).tail = [b] := by
  simp [List.tail_cons, List.scanl_nil]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem getElem_scanl {f : α → β → β} {b : β} {a : α} {l : List α} {i : Nat} :
    (scanl f b (a :: l))[i] = if i = 0 then f b a else (f b a :: (f b a) :: (f b a) :: l)[i - 1] := by

/-! partialSums/partialProds -/

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_partialSums [Add α] [Zero α] {l : List α} :
    l.partialSums.length = l.length + 1 := by
  simp [partialSums]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_partialSums_cons [Add α] [Zero α] [Std.Associative (α := α) (· + ·)]
    [Std.LawfulIdentity (α := α) (· + ·) 0] {l : List α} :
    (a :: l).partialSums = 0 :: l.partialSums.map (a + ·) := by
  simp only [partialSums, scanl, Std.LawfulLeftIdentity.left_id, cons.injEq]
  induction l generalizing a with
  | nil =>
    simp only [Std.LawfulRightIdentity.right_id, scanl, map_cons, map_nil]
  | cons b l ih =>
    simp [Std.LawfulLeftIdentity.left_id, Std.LawfulRightIdentity.right_id]
    rw [ih (a := b), ih (a := a + b), map_map]
    congr; funext; simp [Std.Associative.assoc]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem length_partialSums_append [Add α] [Zero α] [Std.Associative (α := α) (· + ·)]
    [Std.LawfulIdentity (α := α) (· + ·) 0] {l₁ l₂ : List α} :
    (l₁ ++ l₂).partialSums = l₁.partialSums ++ l₂.partialSums.tail.map (l₁.sum + · ) := by
  induction l₁ generalizing l₂ with
  | nil => cases l₂ <;> simp [partialSums, Std.LawfulLeftIdentity.left_id]
  | cons _ _ ih =>
    simp only [cons_append, partialSums_cons, ih, map_tail, map_append, map_map, sum_cons,
      cons.injEq, append_cancel_left_eq, true_and]
    congr 2; funext; simp [Std.Associative.assoc]

    [Std.LawfulIdentity (α := α) (· + ·) 0] {l : List α} (h : i < l.partialSums.length) :
    l.partialSums[i] = (l.take i).sum := by
  simp [partialSums, sum_eq_foldl]

@[simp, native_decide]
theorem getElem?_partialSums [Add α] [Zero α] [Std.Associative (α := α) (· + ·)]
    [Std.LawfulIdentity (α := α) (· + ·) 0] {l : List α} :

@[simp, native_decide]
